title: Browse/filter UI (discipline, grade band, cost, materials, hazard level) — offline
context: oer-science-labs needs a browse and filter interface so educators can find relevant guides quickly. All filtering must work offline (client-side only, no server) since many target users have limited connectivity. The UI must meet WCAG 2.2 AA and print cleanly. This task builds on the static site skeleton (code-005) and is required before i18n (code-014) and the production deployment (code-021).
objective: Build the browse/filter UI for oer-science-labs that allows educators to filter guides by discipline, grade band, cost, available materials, and hazard level. All filter logic must run client-side so it works offline after first visit. The UI must be keyboard-navigable, screen-reader accessible, and render per-guide print output. No telemetry, no tracking, no server calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Guides can be browsed and filtered by discipline, grade band, estimated cost, required materials, and hazard level — all client-side with no server request
Browse and guide-detail views load and function correctly offline after first visit (verified by offline E2E)
UI is keyboard-navigable (focus order, skip-nav, visible focus indicators) and screen-reader accessible (semantic HTML, ARIA where needed); automated axe/pa11y checks pass with 0 critical issues
Per-guide print/PDF output renders a clean, readable, standalone page (no web navigation chrome in print output)
No telemetry, third-party scripts, or unexpected outbound network requests; CSP connect-src 'none' maintained; runtime network-interception E2E passes
UI passes the offline E2E and accessibility CI gates added in code-004
Responsive design works across desktop, tablet, and mobile viewports
output: A pull request adding the browse/filter UI (discipline, grade band, cost, materials, hazard level) to the oer-science-labs static site, with all filter logic running client-side for offline use.
